why: Everyone says 'deadline'; literal Hindi 'antim tithi'/Tamil 'kadaisi theethi' sounds bureaucratic and unnatural in speech.
Spoken Hindi/Tamil both use 'schedule'; 'samay-saarini'/'kala attavanai' are written-register and stiff in dub.
Universal everyday loanword in office speech; no one says 'baithak'/'kootam' casually for a work meeting.
Fixed term for the deliverable; 'prastuti'/'vazhanguthal' don't map to the slide-deck meaning naturally.
Standard spoken term; 'pratikriya'/'karutthu' shift meaning and feel formal/translated.
Genuine native words exist and are used in earnest/motivational contexts; reads naturally.
प्रेरणा/ஊக்கம் are everyday natural words for motivation/inspiration in speech.
मौका and வாய்ப்பு are the natural spoken words; far more common than the English loan.
माहौल (atmosphere/surroundings) and சூழல் are natural; pick the social-environment sense over technical 'paryavaran'.
Both are real, widely-used words, especially in public-message contexts.
ज़िम्मेदारी/பொறுப்பு are core everyday native words; clearly localize.
अनुभव/அனுபவம் are natural and universal for lived experience.
ज्ञान/அறிவு are everyday native words for knowledge.
जिज्ञासा and ஆர்வம் (eagerness/curiosity) are natural spoken words.
आत्मविश्वास/தன்னம்பிக்கை are standard, natural self-confidence words.
नज़रिया and பார்வை (viewpoint) are the natural conversational choices over loaning 'perspective'.
'Attitude' is widely spoken as-is (esp. youth/colloquial); 'rawaiya'/'manappaanmai' are formal and don't carry the casual sense.
व्यवहार/நடத்தை are everyday native words for conduct/behaviour.
फ़ैसला and முடிவு are the natural spoken words for a decision.
तुलना/ஒப்பீடு are natural and commonly used for comparison.
सारांश/சுருக்கம் are real, naturally-used words for a summary/gist.
Students and offices say 'assignment'; literal renders feel academic and unnatural for homework/tasks.
Everyday education loanword; 'paathyakram'/'paadathittam' exist but speech defaults to 'syllabus'.
अवधारणा/கருத்து carry the idea/concept sense naturally in explanatory speech; native words read well.
In study context 'revision' is the spoken default; 'punaravalokan'/'thiruppi padithal' are clunky.
School/office speech uses 'attendance'; 'upasthiti'/'varuhai' are formal-register and rarely spoken casually.
Universal everyday loanword; 'panchang'/'naatkaati' carry traditional-almanac connotations, not the wall/phone calendar.
Phone/app culture made 'reminder' the spoken default; 'anusmaarak'/'gnaabakam' feel literary.
Everyone says 'document'; 'dastavez' is okay but the loan dominates office/everyday speech, Tamil 'aavanam' is formal.
दस्तख़त and கையெழுத்து are the natural everyday words for a signature, used more than 'signature'.
Universal loanword; 'karyalay'/'aluvalagam' are formal-register and 'office' dominates speech.
Everyday loanword; 'vidyalay'/'palli' exist but spoken default is 'school'.
Spoken default everywhere; native coinages are rarely used casually.
'Exam'/'pareeksha'/'thervu' coexist but 'exam' is the casual spoken default among students.
Everyday loanword for a lesson/grade; 'kaksha'/'vakuppu' are formal-register.
Office/student speech uses 'project' universally; 'pariyojana'/'thittam' feel formal or shift meaning.
'Report' is the spoken default in office/news contexts; 'prativedan'/'arikkai' are formal-register.
तनख़्वाह/சம்பளம் are natural everyday words for salary, used more than the English loan.
लक्ष्य/இலக்கு are natural, common words for an aim/goal in motivational and everyday speech.
अनुशासन/ஒழுக்கம் are real, widely-used words for self-discipline.
मेहनत (hard work) and முயற்சி (effort/attempt) are the natural spoken words.
